content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration . . . Executive/Legal . . . Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                          322-5901                    322-6441


August 3, 1982


Senator John Holmdahl
3086 State Capitol
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Senator Holmdahl:

     This is in response to your letter dated August 2, 1982, and
my earlier telephone conversation with a member of your staff, George
Spanos, in which you asked about your semi-annual filing re-
quirement for campaign disclosure statements for both your current
position as a member of the Senate and your appointment and confirm-
ation to the Court of Appeals.

     As stated in your letter, as a member of the Senate you must
file a semi-annual statement disclosing all receipts and expenditures
that have been made pursuant to Government Code Section 84200 which
states in part that:

          ... All elected officers shall file campaign statements no
          later than July 31 and January 31...

     With regard to your judicial appointment, Section 84200 further
states that:

          ...provided that this requirement does not apply to elected
          officers whose salaries are less than one hundred dollars
          ($100) a month or to judges, unless such elected officer or
          judge is a candidate or committee which receives contributions
          or makes expenditures during the applicable six-month period.

     Since you have received no contributions and made no expendi-
tures in connection with the judicial office, you have no require-
ment to file a semi-annual statement for that office.

     If I can be of any further assistance, please do not hesitate
to contact me.

                                        Yours Truly,


                                        Lynn Montgomery
                                        Political Reform
                                        Consultant

PLEASE ADDRESS REPLY TO
OFFICE DESIGNATED

STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO 95814
(916) 445-6671

DISTRICT OFFICE
22100 FOOTHILL BOULEVARD
SUITE 307
HAYWARD, CALIFORNIA 94541
(415) 538-4844

STATE SENATOR
JOHN W. HOLMDAHL
EIGHTH SENATORIAL DISTRICT
ALAMEDA COUNTY

CALIFORNIA LEGISLATURE

Senate

August 2, 1982

Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, California

Attention: Lynn Montgomery

This will confirm the substance of your conversation with a
member of my staff. I am a member of the Senate and in that
capacity have filed a campaign statement covering the first
six months of 1982 and setting forth all receipts and
expenditures I have made.

You were also informed that, during the first six months of 1982,
I was appointed to the Court of Appeal and my appointment was
confirmed. I have received no contributions and made no expenditures in connection with the judicial office, or in connection
with any office except as shown on the statement I filed as a
Senator.

This will confirm that you have indicated that I need not file
an additional statement because of my status as an appointee
to the court.

Your confirmation of this letter is requested.

Yours truly,

JOHN W. HOLMDAHL
H:sn

COMMITTEE
FINANCE
REVENUE AND TAXATION
TRANSPORTATION
JOINT COMMITTEES
STATE ECONOMY, CHAIRMAN
JOINT RULES
ASSETS AND LIABILITIES
CALIFORNIA GOVERNMENT
EMPLOYMENT SERVICES

sections.question: Does Senator John Holmdahl have a semi-annual filing requirement for campaign disclosure statements for his appointment and confirmation to the Court of Appeals, in addition to his filing requirement as a member of the Senate?
sections.conclusion: Since you have received no contributions and made no expenditures in connection with the judicial office, you have no requirement to file a semi-annual statement for that office. With regard to your Senate position, you must file semi-annual statements pursuant to Government Code Section 84200. Section 84200 exempts judges from the filing requirement unless they are candidates or their committees receive contributions or make expenditures during the applicable six-month period.
sections.question_synthetic: A state senator who was appointed and confirmed to the Court of Appeals asks whether he must file separate semi-annual campaign disclosure statements for his judicial position in addition to his Senate filings.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Senator Holmdahl must continue filing semi-annual statements for his Senate position, but he has no filing requirement for his Court of Appeals appointment since he received no contributions and made no expenditures related to that judicial office.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (dated August 2, 1982) and the FPPC response letter (dated August 3, 1982). Both are presented in sequence. Minor typographical errors in original document preserved as written (e.g., 'Holmdahl' appears as 'Ho1mdah1' in one instance).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does Senator John Holmdahl have a semi-annual filing requirement for campaign disclosure statements for his appointment and confirmation to the Court of Appeals, in addition to his filing requirement as a member of the Senate?

CONCLUSION: Since you have received no contributions and made no expenditures in connection with the judicial office, you have no requirement to file a semi-annual statement for that office. With regard to your Senate position, you must file semi-annual statements pursuant to Government Code Section 84200. Section 84200 exempts judges from the filing requirement unless they are candidates or their committees receive contributions or make expenditures during the applicable six-month period.

embedding.summary: FPPC response to Senator John Holmdahl regarding his dual filing obligations as a state senator and Court of Appeals appointee. The FPPC advises that while he must file semi-annual statements for his Senate position, he is exempt from filing for his judicial appointment because he had no campaign activity.
